title: Medical Device Maintenance Slotting
description: A hospital-operations and compliance workflow that enforces control checks while allocating operational resources and closing governance loops. Focus case: medical device maintenance slotting.
assumptions: Each medical_device_asset requires an intake action before assignment of time, resources, or verification steps.
Maintenance timeslots, service stations, consumable_kits, and auxiliary_devices are finite resources and must be reserved before execution.
Regulatory documents and certification records are authoritative and must be attached or referenced prior to final closure of a maintenance case.
Device-class and vendor configuration metadata influence permissible procedures and required resources; compatibility relations are prepopulated.
risks: Resource contention: simultaneous reservations may block high-priority cases if resource allocation/priority rules are not enforced.
Document or credential mismatch: attaching incorrect regulatory_document or certification_record could lead to noncompliant closure.
External contractor unavailability: consuming external_contractor_token without fallback may stall execution and delay closure.
Staging area misallocation: using a staging_area that lacks required spare_component_records or consumables may require rollback and rescheduling.
